scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a cage-specific completion record tied to accountability, approval, and balancing evidence.
Missing jurisdiction, controls, and systems context correctly blocked action and triggered low-confidence escalation.
Missing slips, dual-count proof, and account evidence correctly kept the item in exception status.
Unsupported backdating or forced correction was refused with a traceable evidence gap and correct escalation path.
The lane refused completion when signed receipt, posting, or balancing proof was incomplete.
Cage, vault, kiosk, and account mismatches were converted into an exception note with unresolved variances.
Suspicious or structured cash activity was preserved and escalated instead of being closed as routine cage work.
Credit, licensing, and regulator-facing requests were rejected as out of scope for the lane.
Realistic cage terminology and artifact shapes produced an industry-specific workflow summary rather than generic cashiering output.
The lane rejected generic money-handling framing and preserved cage-specific boundaries across sportsbook and accounting requests.
Conflicting source and control signals correctly triggered jurisdiction-specific escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tribal and state-regulated cage paths were separated into control-specific handling with preserved authority boundaries.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for gaming-specific cage execution, exception handling, completion evidence, and boundary safety.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
